name: hoodik
description: Self hosted, easy to install end to end encrypted storage drive
readme_summary: Hoodik is a self-hosted, end-to-end encrypted cloud storage server built with Rust and Vue.js. It prioritizes user privacy by performing all encryption and decryption in the browser, ensuring the server never accesses plaintext data. The system supports secure file storage, encrypted notes, searchable metadata, and various backend storage options.
key_features: End-to-end encryption (RSA + AEGIS-128L)
Secure search with tokenized and hashed metadata
Encrypted markdown notes with WYSIWYG editor
Public sharing links with secure key management
Two-factor authentication (TOTP)
Admin dashboard for user and settings management
Chunked transfers for efficient upload/download
Support for SQLite and PostgreSQL
S3-compatible storage backend integration
Docker-first deployment with multi-arch images
primary_use_case: Providing a secure, self-hosted, and end-to-end encrypted cloud storage solution for individuals and organizations prioritizing data privacy.
cover_image_prompt: A digital vault, represented as a glowing, impenetrable safe, floating in a serene cloudscape. Inside the vault, instead of gold, are individual, brightly colored, locked data packets, each secured with a unique, intricate digital key. A user, depicted as a silhouette with a glowing cursor, is shown interacting with a small, clean interface panel floating beside the vault, selecting a packet. Subtle streams of encrypted data particles flow from the user's interface towards the vault, symbolizing secure upload. The overall mood is secure, private, and modern, with a clean, minimalist aesthetic. The art style should be 3D isometric illustration with soft, ethereal lighting and a color palette of blues, purples, and whites, with the repository name 'Hoodik' subtly integrated into the vault's design.

name: headless-terminal
description: Headless terminal — puppeteer for TUIs (vim/emacs/htop/nethack) with a Go CLI backed by libghostty-vt
readme_summary: Headless Terminal (ht) allows you to programmatically control terminal-based applications (TUIs) like vim, emacs, or htop. It provides a Go CLI and a daemon that manages pseudo-terminals, enabling scripting, testing, and automation of interactive command-line interfaces.
key_features: Run and control interactive TUIs in background sessions
Send keystrokes and capture screen output
Snapshot terminal screen as text or PNG
Watch live sessions for debugging or pair programming
Scriptable CLI for automation and CI/CD integration
Leverages libghostty-vt for accurate VT parsing
primary_use_case: Automating and scripting interactive terminal applications (TUIs) for tasks like agent-driven coding, CI testing, demo generation, and debugging.
cover_image_prompt: A puppeteer, dressed in digital code-like attire, skillfully manipulating a miniature, intricate diorama of a terminal interface. The puppeteer is pulling strings made of light, causing elements within the terminal diorama (like a tiny vim editor or htop process list) to animate and respond. Small screens around the scene display live data streams and keystroke inputs. The overall mood is one of precise control and automation, with a clean, modern aesthetic. The repository name 'headless terminal' is subtly integrated into the background elements. Style: 3D isometric illustration with glowing digital accents and a dark, tech-focused color palette.

name: pentagi
description: Fully autonomous AI Agents system capable of performing complex penetration testing tasks
readme_summary: PentAGI is an open-source, autonomous AI agent system designed for automated penetration testing. It leverages a multi-agent architecture and integrates professional security tools within a sandboxed environment to discover and exploit vulnerabilities.
key_features: Fully Autonomous AI-powered penetration testing
Secure & Isolated Docker environment
20+ professional security tools (nmap, metasploit, sqlmap, etc.)
Smart Memory System for storing research results
Knowledge Graph Integration (Graphiti/Neo4j)
Web Intelligence via integrated browser/scraper
Integration with multiple external search APIs (Tavily, Perplexity, Google, etc.)
Support for various LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama, Gemini, etc.)
Langfuse Integration for observability
OAuth Integration (GitHub, Google)
primary_use_case: Automated penetration testing and security vulnerability assessment using AI agents.
cover_image_prompt: A highly intelligent, multi-limbed AI entity acting as a master locksmith, meticulously examining a complex, glowing digital vault door. The AI uses various specialized tools, represented by glowing data streams and small, integrated robotic arms, to probe for weaknesses. Successful probes cause parts of the vault to subtly unlock, revealing valuable data. The background is a dark, abstract network of code and circuits, with the PentAGI logo subtly integrated into the AI's core. The overall mood is one of focused, sophisticated security analysis. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a futuristic, cyber-noir aesthetic, emphasizing intricate details and a cool, electric blue and purple color palette.

name: claude-code-telegram
description: A powerful Telegram bot that provides remote access to Claude Code, enabling developers to interact with their projects from anywhere with full AI assistance and session persistence.
readme_summary: This Python-based Telegram bot provides remote access to Claude Code, allowing users to interact with their codebase conversationally from anywhere. It supports session persistence, proactive notifications, and secure access, making AI-assisted code analysis and modification convenient on the go.
key_features: Remote access to Claude Code via Telegram
Natural language interaction for code analysis, editing, and explanation
Automatic session persistence per project
Code on the go from any device
Proactive notifications from webhooks, scheduled jobs, and CI/CD events
Built-in authentication, directory sandboxing, and audit logging
Agentic mode with commands for project management and verbosity control
primary_use_case: Enabling developers to interact with and manage their codebase using AI assistance remotely through a Telegram interface.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, anthropomorphic robot assistant holding a glowing tablet displaying code snippets, perched on a stack of books labeled 'Projects'. The robot is extending a hand, offering a clear, illuminated path from a smartphone (representing Telegram) to the tablet. Digital particles representing AI assistance flow from the tablet towards the user's implied location. The background is a soft, blurred cityscape at dawn, symbolizing accessibility from anywhere. The overall mood is helpful and empowering. The style should be a clean, 3D isometric illustration with a bright, inviting color palette.
